insight: The Netherlands team has seen a positive impact from hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup, gaining momentum in both of their breaks so far. On average, they have increased their momentum by 0.300 minutes after each break, which translates to a noticeable boost in their performance on the field. This increase in momentum has occurred when they were not pressing before the break, suggesting that the brief pause allows them to regroup and refocus, leading to a stronger resurgence in the game. This trend indicates that hydration breaks have been a strategic advantage for the Netherlands, enabling them to come back stronger and more energized after the short pause.
